generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance GantryHorizontalMotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Two motors with associated controllers for horizontal movement of the crane's hoist"
]

instance GantryVerticalMotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Two motors with associated controllers for vertical movement of the crane's hoist"
]

instance CraneElectromagnet : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electromagnet at the end of the hoist for container manipulation"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance SwingAngleEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Swing Angle Encoder for real-time position feedback of the crane's horizontal axis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CranePositionData
]

instance Anemometer : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Anemometer for monitoring wind conditions affecting crane stability"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalConditionsData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance CranePositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionsData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Anemometer
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance GantryKinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Detailed kinematic model representing the crane's mechanical structure and motion dynamics derived from CAD data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo Fusion360CADModel
]

instance TrajectoryGenerationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model using kinematic model to compute optimal paths for container puck movements"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBDatabase
]

instance SimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Component simulating the crane's operational behavior for predictive analysis of trajectories"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBDatabase
]

instance ContinuousValidationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Continuous validation service calculating distance metrics to quantify discrepancies between physical and digital twin trajectories"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBDatabase
]

instance RealTimeSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw real-time sensor data from crane position and environmental conditions"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CranePositionData, EnvironmentalConditionsData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBDatabase
]

instance HistoricalOperationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Stored operational data in TimescaleDB for retrospective analysis of crane movements and environmental conditions"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RealTimeSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBDatabase
]

instance TrajectoryExecutionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Data representing executed trajectories for continuous validation against simulations"
    DTDFVocab:fromData TrajectoryGenerationModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContinuousValidationModel
]

instance SimulationResultsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated operational behavior data generated by the simulation service"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SimulationModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContinuousValidationModel
]

// C11: Enablers
instance TimescaleDBDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "TimescaleDB time-series database for historical data logging and storage of operational data"
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataLoggingService, DataVisualizationService, ContinuousValidationService
]

instance GrafanaDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Grafana dashboard for presenting processed data via time-series traces of sensor readings and control signals"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

instance PythonScripting : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python programming language for scripting services including data processing, visualization, and control algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:enables TrajectoryGeneratorService
]

instance ArduinoController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Arduino platform for embedded controller logic and sensor interfacing"
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicalCraneInterface
]

instance MQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT message broker for inter-service signaling between digital twin components"
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalControlService, HistoricalDataLoggingService, DataVisualizationService, ContinuousValidationService
]

instance Fusion360CADModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Autodesk Fusion360 for CAD modeling of the gantry crane's physical components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GantryKinematicModel
]

// C6: Services
instance OptimalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generates optimal movement trajectories to minimize residual swinging motion during operation, ensuring precise positioning"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalTrajectoryAction
]

instance HistoricalDataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Records and stores operational data in TimescaleDB for retrospective analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalDataInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Presents processed data via Grafana dashboard displaying time-series traces of sensor readings and control signals"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables real-time synchronization and validation between physical and digital twins, supporting fault detection and system evolution"
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationInsight
]

instance TrajectoryGeneratorService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Uses kinematic model to compute optimal paths for container puck movements"
    DTDFVocab:provides TrajectoryGenerationAction
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulates operational behavior for predictive analysis of trajectories"
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OptimalTrajectoryAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated generation of optimal movement trajectories to minimize residual swinging motion"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance HistoricalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Retrospective analysis of operational data stored in TimescaleDB for performance evaluation"
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Time-series visualization of sensor readings and control signals via Grafana dashboard"
]

instance ValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time measurement comparison between physical and digital twin trajectories for fault detection"
]

instance TrajectoryGenerationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated computation of optimal container puck movement paths using kinematic model"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analysis of crane operational behavior through simulation modeling"
]

// C1: Physical System Components
instance GantryCraneMechanicalStructure : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Gantry frame with movable crossbeam and load-bearing elements enabling horizontal and vertical motion"
]

instance CraneController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Dedicated control unit interfacing with sensors and actuators for real-time kinematic adjustments"
]

instance PhysicalCraneInterface : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Python program interfacing with crane's controllers that transmits operational state via PhysicalCraneInterface"
]

instance LabEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains GantryCraneMechanicalStructure, CraneController, PhysicalCraneInterface
]

// C12: Containerization Components
instance DatabaseContainer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "TimescaleDB database container storing all real-time and simulated data"
]

instance DashboardContainer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Grafana dashboard container for presenting processed data"
]

instance TrajectoryGeneratorContainer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Container running the Trajectory Generator Service"
]

instance SimulationContainer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Container running the Simulation Service"
]

instance ContinuousValidationContainer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Container running the Continuous Validation Service"
]

instance MQTTBrokerContainer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Container running the MQTT Broker for inter-service communication"
]

instance ContainerizedSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains DatabaseContainer, DashboardContainer, TrajectoryGeneratorContainer,
    SimulationContainer, ContinuousValidationContainer, MQTTBrokerContainer
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual_to_physical_interaction** is mediated through the **Control Application** and **Physical Crane Interface**. The Control Application generates commands (e.g., trajectory instructions) via the Physical Crane Interface to control the crane's movement. The **Trajectory Generator Service** produces optimal trajectories (e.g., motion paths with minimal residual swinging), which are then relayed through the Control Application. The **Continuous Validation Service** does not directly participate in command execution but monitors post-execution discrepancies. There are no explicit authentication or validation mechanisms for commands in the provided documents; the system relies on the Control Application's direct interface with the Physical Crane Interface for command transmission. Feedback systems are implied through the Logging Application’s real-time data capture, but no explicit confirmation mechanisms (e.g., acknowledgments) or conflict resolution protocols (e.g., priority rules) are described.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar employs a **pre-recorded data-based twinning approach**, where virtual machine images include prerecorded sensor and system data, implying a **batch-oriented twinning model** without real-time synchronization. The system does not specify synchronization frequencies, latency requirements, or temporal granularity for this setup. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar, while not detailing explicit time scales, leverages **Microsoft Azure tools** for runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction, suggesting a **near-real-time or event-driven alignment** between physical sensors (Raspberry-Pi with sensors) and the AutomationML-based digital twin. The **lab-scale gantry crane** system does not explicitly mention twinning time scales, but its context diagram implies **real-time status updates** and **continuous logging** for operational monitoring, though no specific synchronization frequency (e.g., latency thresholds) or granularity is provided. The **ARCHES-PiCar-X** exemplar (not detailed in the provided documents) is inferred to use **ad-hoc real-time coordination** due to its logistics/cyber-physical domain focus, but no concrete time-scale specifics are available."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar employs a **low-cost, ad-hoc architecture** using **InfluxDB (time-series database)** and **ActiveMQ (message broker)** for real-time synchronization, with **virtual machines** hosting the digital twin. The **Air Quality Management** system leverages **AutomationML (standardized digital twin representation)** and **Microsoft Azure tools** for cloud-based orchestration, supporting **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction** via a **centralized twin instance**. The **lab-scale gantry crane** system uses **containerization (six containers)** with **MQTT broker** for inter-service communication, implementing a **decentralized coordination pattern** where each container (e.g., Trajectory Generator, Simulation) operates independently but synchronizes via **MQTT signaling**. Orchestration is abstracted via the **C4 Model** for architecture visualization, though no explicit orchestration framework is detailed. All exemplars avoid commercial digital twin frameworks, opting for **ad-hoc, technology-specific solutions**."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The provided Digital Twin exemplars do not explicitly define formal lifecycle stages or governance models. Instead, they adopt an **ad-hoc, technology-driven approach** without structured lifecycle phases such as 'Early modeling,' 'Conceptual phase,' or 'Transition management.'

**Key Observations:**
- **GreenhouseDT** uses **virtual machines (VMs)** with pre-recorded data for deployment, implying a deployment-focused lifecycle stage rather than a formalized one.
- **Air Quality Management** leverages **AutomationML-based digital twins** and **Microsoft Azure tools**, suggesting a pipeline-driven lifecycle where simulation, prediction, and runtime visualization are treated as continuous operational phases rather than discrete stages.
- **Gantry Crane** (implied by the discussion) uses **containerization or virtualization** for modular dissemination, indicating an iterative development and deployment lifecycle without explicit documentation of lifecycle phases.

No explicit lifecycle governance frameworks (e.g., ISO 14224, IEEE 15288) are referenced, reinforcing the **ad-hoc nature** of these approaches.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The **Lab Scale Gantry Crane** employs:
- **Validation methodology**: A **Continuous Validation Service** that compares sensor-measured trajectory execution against simulated predictions, calculating distance-based error metrics.
- **Uncertainty quantification**: Implicitly addressed through trajectory error metrics derived from simulation vs. real-time data comparisons.
- **Quality assurance**: No explicit mention of formal validation frameworks (e.g., Bayesian inference), but the **Continuous Validation Service** ensures real-time fidelity checks.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **digital twin’s technical network infrastructure** operates on a **single personal computer** hosting the system. The **MQTT Broker** serves as the primary communication protocol for inter-service signaling between containers (e.g., Trajectory Generator Service, Simulation Service, Continuous Validation Service). The **Database (TimescaleDB)** stores logged data, while the **Dashboard (Grafana)** visualizes metrics. No explicit network topology, bandwidth requirements, or latency constraints are specified. Security protocols are not mentioned, and redundancy mechanisms are absent in the described architecture. The system does not leverage cloud/edge/on-premise hosting beyond the local PC environment.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **digital twin deployment** is hosted on a **single personal computer**, utilizing **Docker containers** for the Database, MQTT Broker, and Dashboard. The **TimescaleDB** database is the sole storage solution, with no mention of distributed storage or horizontal scaling. Deployment models supported include **containerized software stacks** (as per the C4 Model’s container diagram). The system does not specify computational/storage requirements, availability guarantees, or reliability assurances beyond operational continuity on the host PC. No mention of cloud/edge deployment or scalability mechanisms (e.g., load balancing) is provided in the documents.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** system integrates **physically with a low-cost greenhouse** via virtualized environments and prerecorded data, relying on **InfluxDB** and **ActiveMQ** for internal data exchange. There is **no explicit mention of external system integration** (e.g., IoT platforms, enterprise systems). The **Air Quality Management** exemplar integrates with **Raspberry-Pi sensors** for physical data collection and connects to **Microsoft Azure** for cloud-based processing, simulation, and prediction, adhering to **AutomationML** for interoperability with construction engineering systems. The **lab-scale gantry crane** system interfaces with **crane controllers** (physical interface) via unspecified protocols (e.g., not HTTP), with no evidence of external IoT platform dependencies. The **ARCHES-PiCar-X** exemplar (not detailed) likely integrates with **single-board computers (e.g., Raspberry-Pi)** for real-time control and may connect to **external cyber-physical systems** (e.g., logistics networks) via ad-hoc protocols, though no specifics are provided."
]
